name: Patient Health Questionnaire-9
description: Nine-item depression screening tool derived from the PRIME-MD instrument, measuring the DSM-IV criteria for major depressive disorder. Widely used in primary care and research settings for both screening and severity assessment.
license_detail: Public Domain — Pfizer Inc. has released PHQ-9 into the public domain; no permission required to reproduce, translate, display or distribute.

items.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

name: Patient Health Questionnaire-2
description: Ultra-brief two-item depression screener comprising the first two items of the PHQ-9. Used as an initial screen; a score of 3 or higher warrants administration of the full PHQ-9.
license_detail: Public Domain

items.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless

name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder 7-item scale
description: Seven-item anxiety screening tool measuring the frequency of core anxiety symptoms over the prior two weeks. Validated for generalized anxiety disorder screening and as a severity measure in primary care and research settings.
license_detail: Public Domain — Pfizer Inc. has released GAD-7 into the public domain.

items.text: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

name: Beck Depression Inventory, Second Edition
description: Twenty-one item self-report inventory measuring the severity of depressive symptoms in adults and adolescents aged 13 and older, aligned with DSM-IV diagnostic criteria for major depressive disorder.
license_detail: Copyright Pearson Education. Requires purchase and licensing. Do not reproduce without permission.

name: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale
description: Clinician-administered scale assessing suicidal ideation and behavior across five ideation categories and four behavior categories. Developed at Columbia University and mandated by the FDA for clinical trials involving psychiatric endpoints.

scoring_method.note: Worst ideation type (1-5) is the primary ideation score; behavior subscale is event-based

license_detail: Free for use with acknowledgment of Columbia University. Available at cssrs.columbia.edu.

name: Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale
description: Thirty-item clinician-rated scale for assessing symptom severity in schizophrenia and related psychotic disorders, comprising positive symptom, negative symptom, and general psychopathology subscales.
license_detail: Copyright Multi-Health Systems (MHS). Requires licensing for use.

name: Scale for the Assessment of Positive Symptoms
description: Clinician-administered 34-item scale developed by Nancy Andreasen to assess the severity of positive symptoms in schizophrenia, including hallucinations, delusions, bizarre behavior, and positive formal thought disorder.
license_detail: Available from the University of Iowa; free to use with attribution to Dr. Nancy Andreasen.

name: Scale for the Assessment of Negative Symptoms
description: Clinician-administered 25-item scale developed by Nancy Andreasen to quantify the severity of negative symptoms in schizophrenia, including affective flattening, alogia, avolition, anhedonia, and attention deficits.
license_detail: Available from the University of Iowa; free to use with attribution to Dr. Nancy Andreasen.

name: PTSD Checklist for DSM-5
description: Twenty-item self-report measure assessing DSM-5 symptoms of PTSD across four symptom clusters: intrusion, avoidance, negative alterations in cognition and mood, and alterations in arousal and reactivity. Developed and released publicly by the National Center for PTSD.

interpretation.note: Score of 31-33+ is suggestive of PTSD; confirm with clinical interview

license_detail: Public domain — published by the US Department of Veterans Affairs National Center for PTSD.

name: Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test
description: Ten-item WHO-developed screening tool for hazardous and harmful alcohol use and early identification of alcohol use disorders. Covers consumption (items 1-3), dependence symptoms (items 4-6), and harmful alcohol use (items 7-10).

scoring_method.note: Items 1-8 scored 0,1,2,3,4; items 9-10 scored 0,2,4 only

license_detail: World Health Organization. Free to use with attribution to WHO.

items.text: How often do you have a drink containing alcohol?
How many drinks containing alcohol do you have on a typical day when you are drinking?
How often do you have six or more drinks on one occasion?
How often during the last year have you found that you were not able to stop drinking once you had started?
How often during the last year have you failed to do what was normally expected from you because of drinking?
How often during the last year have you needed a first drink in the morning to get yourself going after a heavy drinking session?
How often during the last year have you had a feeling of guilt or remorse after drinking?
How often during the last year have you been unable to remember what happened the night before because you had been drinking?
Have you or someone else been injured as a result of your drinking?

options.label: Yes, but not in the last year

items.text: Has a relative or friend, or a doctor or another health worker, been concerned about your drinking or suggested that you cut down?

options.label: Yes, but not in the last year

name: Drug Abuse Screening Test-10
description: Ten-item self-report questionnaire screening for problematic drug use (excluding alcohol and tobacco) over the past 12 months. Adapted from the original 28-item DAST and validated across clinical and community populations.
license_detail: Harvey A. Skinner; freely available for clinical and research use.

name: CAGE Alcohol Questionnaire
description: Four-item brief alcohol misuse screening questionnaire used in primary care. The acronym CAGE stands for Cut down, Annoyed, Guilty, Eye-opener. A score of 2 or more is considered clinically significant.
license_detail: John Ewing; published in JAMA 1984. Public domain.

items.text: Have you ever felt you should Cut down on your drinking?
Have people Annoyed you by criticizing your drinking?
Have you ever felt bad or Guilty about your drinking?
Have you ever had a drink first thing in the morning to steady your nerves or to get rid of a hangover (Eye-opener)?

name: Alcohol, Smoking and Substance Involvement Screening Test
description: Eight-item WHO-developed screening tool assessing lifetime and recent use of tobacco, alcohol, cannabis, cocaine, amphetamines, inhalants, sedatives, hallucinogens, opioids, and other drugs, as well as associated problems and dependence indicators.

interpretation.27+: High risk — referral for assessment and treatment

license_detail: World Health Organization. Freely available at who.int.

name: Substance Abuse Subtle Screening Inventory-3
description: Ninety-three item self-report screening instrument with both direct (face-valid) and indirect (subtle) subscales for identifying individuals with high probability of a substance use disorder, including an empirically derived defensiveness scale.
license_detail: Copyright SASSI Institute. Requires purchase and certification for administration.

name: EuroQol 5-Dimension 3-Level
description: Standardized five-dimension health status instrument with three response levels (no problems, some problems, extreme problems) plus a visual analogue scale. Generates a single index value for health state utility in cost-effectiveness analyses.

scoring_method.note: Index value derived from population-based value sets; VAS scored 0-100

license_detail: Copyright EuroQol Research Foundation. Requires registration and license at euroqol.org. Free for academic non-commercial use.

name: EuroQol 5-Dimension 5-Level
description: Updated version of the EQ-5D with five response levels per dimension (no, slight, moderate, severe, extreme problems) offering greater sensitivity and reduced ceiling effects compared to the 3L version, plus a visual analogue scale.

scoring_method.note: Crosswalk value sets available; VAS scored 0-100

license_detail: Copyright EuroQol Research Foundation. Requires registration and license at euroqol.org.

name: Short Form Health Survey-36
description: Thirty-six item generic health survey measuring eight health domains: physical functioning, role-physical, bodily pain, general health, vitality, social functioning, role-emotional, and mental health. Produces Physical Component Summary and Mental Component Summary scores.

scoring_method.note: Each scale scored 0-100; summary PCS and MCS computed via norm-based scoring

license_detail: Copyright RAND Corporation / QualityMetric. Requires licensing for commercial use. RAND 36-item version is free for non-commercial use.

name: Short Form Health Survey-12
description: Twelve-item abbreviated version of the SF-36 designed for large-scale population surveys where respondent burden is a concern. Produces Physical Component Summary (PCS-12) and Mental Component Summary (MCS-12) scores.

scoring_method.note: Norm-based T-score scoring; population mean = 50, SD = 10

license_detail: Copyright QualityMetric. Requires licensing.

name: WHO Quality of Life — Brief
description: Twenty-six item abbreviated version of the WHOQOL-100 measuring quality of life across four domains: physical health, psychological health, social relationships, and environment. Developed cross-culturally by the WHO and validated in 15 international field centers.

scoring_method.note: Two items (overall QoL and health satisfaction) not included in domain scores

license_detail: World Health Organization. Freely available for non-commercial use from who.int.

name: Health Utilities Index
description: Generic preference-based health status classification system measuring functional capacity across multiple attributes including vision, hearing, speech, ambulation, dexterity, emotion, cognition, and pain. Available in HUI2 (7 attributes) and HUI3 (8 attributes) versions.

scoring_method.note: Multiplicative multi-attribute utility function; dead = 0, perfect health = 1.00

license_detail: Copyright Health Utilities Inc. Requires licensing at healthutilities.com.

name: Short Form 6-Dimension
description: Six-dimension preference-based health status measure derived from the SF-36 using psychometric selection of eleven items from six dimensions. Converts SF-36 profile scores into a single utility index value for economic evaluation.

scoring_method.note: Additive and multiplicative scoring algorithms available; population-based preference weights

license_detail: Copyright Medical Outcomes Trust / John Brazier. Requires licensing.

name: Visual Analogue Scale — Pain
description: Single-item continuous scale for patient self-rating of pain intensity, presented as a 100mm horizontal line anchored at 'no pain' (0) and 'worst imaginable pain' (100). Widely used in clinical trials and acute care settings.

scoring_method.note: Measured in mm from the 'no pain' end; minimal clinically important difference approximately 9-11mm

license_detail: Public domain. Original methodology by Scott and Huskisson (1976).

name: Numeric Rating Scale — Pain
description: Single-item verbal or written 11-point scale (0-10) for pain intensity assessment where 0 = no pain and 10 = worst possible pain. The most widely used pain intensity scale in clinical practice due to its brevity and ease of administration.
license_detail: Public domain.

name: Brief Pain Inventory
description: Seventeen-item multidimensional pain assessment tool evaluating pain severity (worst, least, average, current) and pain interference with seven functional domains including general activity, mood, walking, normal work, relations with others, sleep, and enjoyment of life.

scoring_method.note: Severity score = mean of 4 severity items; Interference score = mean of 7 interference items; both on 0-10 NRS

license_detail: Charles Cleeland / University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center. Free for use; available at mdanderson.org.

name: McGill Pain Questionnaire
description: Seventy-eight item multidimensional pain assessment instrument measuring sensory, affective, evaluative, and miscellaneous pain qualities through a set of 20 subgroups of pain descriptors, plus a Present Pain Intensity scale.
license_detail: Copyright Ronald Melzack / Elsevier. Requires permission for reproduction.

name: Short Form McGill Pain Questionnaire
description: Fifteen-item abbreviated version of the McGill Pain Questionnaire consisting of eleven sensory and four affective descriptors rated on a 4-point intensity scale, plus a VAS and Present Pain Intensity item. The SF-MPQ-2 extends this to neuropathic pain.
license_detail: Copyright Ronald Melzack / Elsevier. Requires permission.

name: Barthel Index of Activities of Daily Living
description: Ten-item functional assessment scale measuring independence in basic activities of daily living including feeding, bathing, grooming, dressing, bowel and bladder control, toilet use, transfers, mobility, and stair climbing. Originally developed for rehabilitation settings.

scoring_method.note: Items weighted differently; scores in increments of 5

license_detail: Mahoney and Barthel (1965). Published in the Maryland State Medical Journal; public domain.

items.text: Feeding

options.label: Needs help cutting, spreading butter, etc., or requires modified diet
Independent. Food provided within reach; able to eat in reasonable time

items.text: Bathing
Grooming

options.label: Needs to personal care. Face/hair/teeth/shaving (implements provided)

items.text: Dressing

options.label: Needs help but can do about half unaided
Independent. Including buttons, zips, laces, etc.

items.text: Bowel control

options.label: Incontinent (or needs to be given enemas)

items.text: Bladder control

options.label: Incontinent, or catheterized and unable to manage alone

items.text: Toilet use

options.label: Needs some help, but can do something alone

items.text: Transfers (bed to chair and back)

options.label: Major help (one or two people, physical), can sit

items.text: Mobility (on level surfaces)

options.label: Walks with help of one person (verbal or physical) > 50 yards
Independent (but may use any aid; e.g., stick) > 50 yards

items.text: Stairs

name: Katz Index of Independence in Activities of Daily Living
description: Six-item assessment of functional independence in basic self-care activities: bathing, dressing, toileting, transferring, continence, and feeding. Commonly used in geriatric assessment, long-term care, and outcomes research.
license_detail: Katz et al. (1963). Published in JAMA; public domain.

name: Lawton Instrumental Activities of Daily Living Scale
description: Eight-item scale assessing independence in more complex activities of daily living including ability to use telephone, shopping, food preparation, housekeeping, laundry, transportation, medication management, and financial management.

scoring_method.note: Each item scored 0 (dependent) or 1 (independent); sex-specific norms available for some items

license_detail: Lawton and Brody (1969). Published in Gerontologist; public domain.

name: Functional Independence Measure
description: Eighteen-item clinician-rated functional assessment measuring motor (13 items) and cognitive (5 items) burden of care across self-care, sphincter control, transfers, locomotion, communication, and social cognition domains. Standard outcome measure in inpatient rehabilitation.

scoring_method.note: Each item scored 1-7 (1=total assist, 7=complete independence)

license_detail: Copyright Uniform Data System for Medical Rehabilitation (UDSMR). Requires licensing.

name: Oswestry Disability Index
description: Ten-item self-administered questionnaire measuring functional disability in patients with low back pain. Assesses pain intensity, personal care, lifting, walking, sitting, standing, sleeping, sex life, social life, and traveling.

scoring_method.note: Score = (total score / 50) × 100%; if one section missed, score = (total/45) × 100%

license_detail: Fairbank et al. (1980). Published in Physiotherapy; public domain for clinical/research use.

name: Karnofsky Performance Scale
description: Clinician-rated single-item performance scale measuring functional status on an 11-point scale from 100 (normal, no evidence of disease) to 0 (dead), in 10-point increments. Developed for oncology and widely used to determine patient prognosis and ability to tolerate treatment.

interpretation.90: Able to carry on normal activity; minor signs or symptoms
interpretation.70: Cares for self; unable to carry on normal activity
interpretation.50: Requires considerable assistance and frequent medical care
interpretation.40: Disabled; requires special care and assistance
interpretation.20: Very sick; active supportive treatment necessary

license_detail: Karnofsky and Burchenal (1949). Public domain.

name: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status
description: Clinician-rated single-item 6-point scale (0-5) measuring cancer patients' functional performance and ability to tolerate treatment, from fully active (0) to dead (5). The most widely used performance status scale in oncology clinical trials.

interpretation.1: Restricted in physically strenuous activity; ambulatory, light work
interpretation.2: Ambulatory, self-care, no work; up > 50% of waking hours
interpretation.3: Limited self-care; confined to bed/chair > 50% of waking hours
interpretation.4: Completely disabled; no self-care; confined to bed/chair

license_detail: ECOG (Oken et al., 1982). Published in Am J Clin Oncol; public domain.

items.text: What is the patient's ECOG performance status?

options.label: Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease activities without restriction
Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature
Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities; up and about more than 50% of waking hours
Capable of only limited self-care; confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours
Completely disabled; cannot carry on any self-care; totally confined to bed or chair

name: Roland-Morris Disability Questionnaire
description: Twenty-four item self-administered questionnaire measuring physical disability due to low back pain. Items are taken from the Sickness Impact Profile with a phrase 'because of my back' added to each. Scores the number of disability statements endorsed.

scoring_method.note: Each item scored 0 (not checked) or 1 (checked); higher scores indicate greater disability

license_detail: Roland and Morris (1983). Published in Spine; free for non-commercial clinical and research use.

name: Neck Disability Index
description: Ten-section self-report questionnaire measuring functional disability due to neck pain across daily activities including pain intensity, personal care, lifting, reading, headaches, concentration, work, driving, sleeping, and recreation.
license_detail: Vernon and Mior (1991). Copyright Vernon; requires permission for commercial use.

name: European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire Core 30
description: Thirty-item cancer-specific quality of life questionnaire comprising five functional scales, three symptom scales, a global health status/QoL scale, and six single items. The core instrument used in most EORTC cancer trials, supplemented by disease-specific modules.
license_detail: Copyright EORTC. Free for use in non-commercial academic research with registration at eortc.org.

name: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy — General
description: Twenty-seven item cancer-specific quality of life instrument covering four domains: physical well-being, social/family well-being, emotional well-being, and functional well-being. Serves as the core questionnaire for the FACIT measurement system with numerous disease-specific subscales.
license_detail: Copyright FACIT.org. Free for clinical research with registration. Commercial use requires licensing.

name: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy — Fatigue
description: Thirteen-item fatigue subscale from the FACIT measurement system assessing the impact of fatigue and its consequences on daily activities and function in patients with chronic illness and cancer. Can be used as a standalone scale or added to the FACT-G.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate less fatigue; cutpoint of 30 suggests clinically significant fatigue

license_detail: Copyright FACIT.org. Free for clinical research with registration.

name: Edmonton Symptom Assessment System
description: Ten-item symptom rating scale designed for repeated assessment of nine common cancer symptoms (pain, tiredness, drowsiness, nausea, lack of appetite, shortness of breath, depression, anxiety, well-being) plus one patient-defined symptom, each rated on 0-10 NRS.

scoring_method.note: Each item 0-10; TSDS = sum of all 9 standard items (0-90)

license_detail: Bruera et al. (1991). Published by the Alberta Cancer Board; freely available.

name: Expanded Prostate Cancer Index Composite-26
description: Twenty-six item health-related quality of life instrument specific to prostate cancer measuring urinary, bowel, sexual, and hormonal domains. The abbreviated version of the 50-item EPIC, designed for use in routine clinical care and research.
license_detail: Wei et al. (2000). Published in J Urol; University of Michigan. Free for academic use.

name: EORTC Quality of Life Questionnaire Breast Cancer 23
description: Twenty-three item breast cancer-specific supplementary module to the EORTC QLQ-C30, assessing symptoms from systemic therapy, arm symptoms, breast symptoms, body image, sexual functioning, sexual enjoyment, and future perspective.
license_detail: Copyright EORTC. Free for non-commercial academic research with registration.

name: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy — Anemia
description: Forty-item instrument (FACT-G plus 20-item Anemia subscale) measuring quality of life in patients with cancer-related anemia, evaluating fatigue, weakness, and functional impairment due to low hemoglobin levels.
license_detail: Copyright FACIT.org. Free for clinical research with registration.

name: Montreal Cognitive Assessment
description: Thirty-point cognitive screening tool assessing multiple cognitive domains including attention and concentration, executive functions, memory, language, visuoconstructional skills, conceptual thinking, calculations, and orientation. Requires approximately 10 minutes to administer.

scoring_method.note: Add 1 point if ≤12 years of education; cutoff ≥26 for normal

license_detail: Copyright Z. Nasreddine MD. Free for clinical use; commercial/research use requires licensing at mocatest.org.

name: Mini-Mental State Examination
description: Thirty-point clinician-administered cognitive screening test assessing orientation, registration, attention and calculation, recall, language, and visuospatial ability. The most widely used cognitive screening instrument worldwide, though it has known floor effects and educational bias.
license_detail: Copyright Psychological Assessment Resources (PAR). Requires licensing. Previously fee-waived but now requires purchase.

name: NIH Toolbox Cognition Battery
description: Twelve-test battery of computerized cognitive assessments developed by the NIH measuring episodic memory, executive function, attention, processing speed, working memory, and language. Normed across the lifespan (ages 3-85) for use in population-level research.

scoring_method.note: Age-adjusted T-scores with mean 50, SD 10; fully-corrected scores adjust for age, sex, race/ethnicity, and education

license_detail: National Institutes of Health. Free for non-commercial research; available through Healthmeasures.net.

name: Geriatric Depression Scale
description: Fifteen-item (short form) yes/no self-report scale for depression screening in older adults, adapted from the original 30-item GDS to minimize burden. Avoids somatic items that may overlap with physical illness in geriatric populations.
license_detail: Yesavage et al. (1982/1986). Available from Stanford University; free for clinical and research use.

name: FRAIL Scale
description: Five-item clinical questionnaire screening for frailty in older adults across five components: fatigue, resistance (stair climbing), ambulation (walking), illness burden, and loss of weight. Simple enough for use in busy clinical settings without performance testing.
license_detail: Morley et al. (2012). Published in J Nutr Health Aging; free to use.

name: Tinetti Performance-Oriented Mobility Assessment
description: Clinician-administered 28-item gait and balance assessment evaluating sitting balance, arising from chair, standing balance, turning, sitting down, and gait quality including step length, symmetry, continuity, path deviation, trunk stability, and walking stance.
license_detail: Tinetti (1986). Published in JAGS; public domain.

name: Falls Efficacy Scale
description: Ten-item self-efficacy scale measuring fear of falling and confidence in performing activities of daily living without falling. Assesses confidence in performing ten specified activities on a 10-point scale from 'very confident' to 'not confident at all'.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate greater fear of falling; FES-I is the updated international version with 16 items

license_detail: Tinetti et al. (1990). Copyright; requires permission for commercial use. FES-I freely available from ProFaNE.

name: Older Americans Resources and Services Multidimensional Functional Assessment Questionnaire
description: Comprehensive multidimensional assessment of older adults covering social resources, economic resources, mental health, physical health, and activities of daily living across approximately 100 items. Developed at Duke University for clinical and population research.

scoring_method.note: Each domain scored 1-6 (1=excellent, 6=totally impaired); summary Cumulative Impairment Score available

license_detail: Duke University Center for the Study of Aging (1978). Free for non-commercial use.

name: Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire
description: Twenty-three item disease-specific health status measure for heart failure patients assessing physical limitations, symptoms (frequency, severity, recent change), quality of life, and social interference. Produces Overall Summary Score (OSS) and Clinical Summary Score (CSS).

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate better health status; ≥5 point change considered clinically meaningful

license_detail: Copyright John Spertus / Saint Luke's Health System. Requires licensing at cvoutcomes.org.

name: Seattle Angina Questionnaire
description: Nineteen-item disease-specific health status measure for patients with coronary artery disease assessing physical limitations, angina stability, angina frequency, treatment satisfaction, and disease perception/quality of life.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate better health status

license_detail: Copyright John Spertus. Requires licensing.

name: Minnesota Living with Heart Failure Questionnaire
description: Twenty-one item self-administered disease-specific quality of life questionnaire for heart failure patients measuring physical and emotional dimensions of how heart failure limits daily activities and causes symptoms over the past month.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate worse quality of life; ≥5 point change clinically meaningful

license_detail: Copyright Regents of the University of Minnesota. Requires licensing.

name: MacNew Heart Disease Health-Related Quality of Life Instrument
description: Twenty-seven item self-administered quality of life measure for patients with ischemic heart disease assessing emotional, physical, and social functioning limitations related to heart disease during the previous two weeks.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate better quality of life; MCID approximately 0.5 points

license_detail: Copyright MacNew Heart Disease QoL Group. Contact for licensing.

name: COPD Assessment Test
description: Eight-item self-administered health status questionnaire for patients with COPD measuring cough, phlegm, chest tightness, breathlessness on hills/stairs, activity limitations at home, confidence leaving home, sleep, and energy.
license_detail: GlaxoSmithKline. Free for use in clinical practice and non-commercial research. Available at catestonline.org.

name: Modified Medical Research Council Dyspnea Scale
description: Single-item ordinal scale (0-4) measuring the degree of breathlessness during daily activities in patients with COPD and other respiratory conditions. Grades dyspnea from only with strenuous exercise (grade 0) to too breathless to leave the house (grade 4).

interpretation.1: Short of breath when hurrying or walking up a slight hill
interpretation.2: Walks slower than people of same age or stops for breath every 100 yards
interpretation.3: Stops for breath after 100 yards or after a few minutes on level ground
interpretation.4: Too breathless to leave the house or breathless when dressing/undressing

license_detail: Public domain. Based on Fletcher et al. (1959), modified by the Medical Research Council.

name: St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire
description: Fifty-item disease-specific health status questionnaire for patients with chronic airflow limitation measuring symptom frequency and severity, activity limitations, and social and psychological impact of respiratory disease.

scoring_method.note: Lower scores indicate better health status; MCID = 4 units

license_detail: Copyright St. George's Hospital Medical School (Paul Jones). Free for research; requires permission.

name: Asthma Control Test
description: Five-item patient self-completed questionnaire assessing asthma control over the past four weeks based on daily activities, dyspnea, nocturnal symptoms, reliever use, and overall asthma control rating.
license_detail: QualityMetric / AstraZeneca. Free for clinical use.

name: Problem Areas in Diabetes
description: Twenty-item self-report scale measuring emotional distress related to living with diabetes, covering concerns about diet, hypoglycemia, social support, complications, and the emotional burden of the disease itself.

scoring_method.note: Raw sum multiplied by 1.25 to produce 0-100 scale

license_detail: Polonsky et al. (1995). Copyright; contact Joslin Diabetes Center for permission.

name: Diabetes Distress Scale
description: Seventeen-item self-report scale assessing diabetes-related distress across four subscales: emotional burden, physician-related distress, regimen-related distress, and interpersonal distress, all on a 6-point Likert scale.
license_detail: Polonsky et al. (2005). Published in Diabetes Care; freely available.

name: Diabetes Treatment Satisfaction Questionnaire
description: Eight-item questionnaire assessing diabetes patients' satisfaction with current treatment regimen, including perceived frequency of hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia, willingness to continue current treatment, flexibility, convenience, and understanding of diabetes.

scoring_method.note: Hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia items not included in total satisfaction score

license_detail: Copyright Clare Bradley / Royal Holloway University of London. Requires licensing.

name: Diabetes Self-Management Questionnaire — Revised
description: Sixteen-item self-report measure of diabetes self-management behaviors including glucose management, dietary control, physical activity, healthcare use, and self-monitoring frequency, revised for use across both type 1 and type 2 diabetes.
license_detail: Schmitt et al. / German Diabetes Center. Contact for use.

name: Pediatric Quality of Life Inventory
description: Twenty-three item multidimensional generic pediatric quality of life instrument measuring physical, emotional, social, and school functioning in children ages 2-18 and young adults ages 18-25. Available in parallel child self-report and parent proxy-report versions.
license_detail: Copyright James W. Varni. Requires licensing at pedsql.org.

name: Child Behavior Checklist
description: One hundred-item parent-report behavior checklist for children ages 6-18 assessing a wide range of behavioral and emotional problems and competencies across eight syndrome scales and DSM-oriented scales. Part of the Achenbach System of Empirically Based Assessment (ASEBA).

scoring_method.note: T-scores normalized to population; T ≥ 70 = clinical range; T 65-69 = borderline clinical

license_detail: Copyright Thomas Achenbach / ASEBA. Requires licensing at aseba.org.

name: Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire
description: Twenty-five item behavioral screening questionnaire for children ages 2-17 covering emotional symptoms, conduct problems, hyperactivity/inattention, peer relationship problems, and prosocial behavior. Available in parent, teacher, and self-report versions with impact supplement.

scoring_method.note: Prosocial scale not included in Total Difficulties score

license_detail: Copyright Robert Goodman. Free for non-commercial use. Available at sdqinfo.org.

name: Pediatric Symptom Checklist-17
description: Seventeen-item parent-completed psychosocial screener for children ages 4-16 assessing attention, internalizing, and externalizing behavioral problems. Abbreviated from the 35-item PSC for use in primary care settings.
license_detail: Jellinek and Murphy. Free for non-commercial use; available from Massachusetts General Hospital.

name: National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale
description: Eleven-item clinician-administered neurological examination quantifying stroke severity across consciousness, vision, facial palsy, motor function (arm and leg), ataxia, sensation, language/aphasia, dysarthria, and inattention/neglect.
license_detail: Developed by NIH/NINDS. Public domain. Available at nihstrokescale.org.

name: Modified Rankin Scale
description: Single-item ordinal scale (0-6) measuring the degree of disability and dependence in daily activities following stroke or other neurological conditions. The primary outcome measure in most stroke clinical trials worldwide.

interpretation.2: Slight disability; unable to carry out all previous activities but independent
interpretation.3: Moderate disability; requires some help, able to walk unassisted
interpretation.4: Moderately severe disability; unable to walk without assistance and unable to attend to bodily needs without assistance
interpretation.5: Severe disability; bedridden, incontinent, requires constant nursing care and attention

license_detail: Rankin (1957), modified by van Swieten (1988). Public domain.

items.text: What is the patient's modified Rankin Scale score?

options.label: No significant disability despite symptoms; able to carry out all usual duties and activities
Slight disability; unable to carry out all previous activities, but able to look after own affairs without assistance
Moderate disability requiring some help, but able to walk without assistance
Moderately severe disability; unable to walk without assistance and unable to attend to own bodily needs without assistance
Severe disability; bedridden, incontinent, and requiring constant nursing care and attention

name: Glasgow Coma Scale
description: Three-component neurological scale assessing eye opening, verbal response, and motor response to evaluate level of consciousness in acute brain injury. The most widely used clinical scale for assessing and communicating consciousness level in emergency and critical care settings.

scoring_method.note: Scores reported as GCS E+V+M (e.g., E3V4M6 = GCS 13)

license_detail: Teasdale and Jennett (1974). University of Glasgow. Public domain; available at glasgowcomascale.org.

items.text: Eye Opening Response
Best Verbal Response
Best Motor Response

name: Full Outline of Unresponsiveness Score
description: Four-component neurological scale assessing eye response, motor response, brainstem reflexes, and respiration pattern in critically ill patients. Unlike the GCS, detects locked-in syndrome and gradations within 'deep coma' and can be used in intubated patients.

scoring_method.note: Score of 0 in all components indicates brain death

license_detail: Wijdicks et al. (2005). Mayo Clinic. Published in Mayo Clinic Proceedings; free for clinical use.

name: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index
description: Nineteen-item self-report questionnaire assessing sleep quality and disturbances over the past month, yielding seven component scores and a global score. Components include subjective sleep quality, latency, duration, efficiency, disturbances, use of sleeping medication, and daytime dysfunction.
license_detail: Buysse et al. (1989). University of Pittsburgh. Free for clinical and non-commercial research use.

name: Epworth Sleepiness Scale
description: Eight-item self-report questionnaire measuring daytime sleepiness by asking respondents to rate the chance of dozing in eight different sitting situations. Widely used to screen for sleep disorders including obstructive sleep apnea and narcolepsy.
license_detail: Murray Johns (1991). Epworth Sleep Centre. Free for non-commercial use; available at epworthsleepinessscale.com.

items.text: Sitting and reading
Watching TV
Sitting inactive in a public place (e.g., a theatre or a meeting)
As a passenger in a car for an hour without a break
Lying down to rest in the afternoon when circumstances permit
Sitting and talking to someone
Sitting quietly after a lunch without alcohol
In a car, while stopped for a few minutes in traffic

name: Insomnia Severity Index
description: Seven-item self-report questionnaire assessing the nature, severity, and impact of insomnia over the past two weeks, including difficulties initiating and maintaining sleep, early morning awakening, sleep dissatisfaction, interference with daily functioning, and distress.
license_detail: Charles Morin. Copyright; contact Morin Lab for permission. Free for academic non-commercial use.

name: International Index of Erectile Function
description: Fifteen-item self-report questionnaire assessing male sexual function across five domains: erectile function, orgasmic function, sexual desire, intercourse satisfaction, and overall satisfaction. The reference standard for erectile dysfunction assessment in clinical trials.
license_detail: Rosen et al. (1997). Copyright; requires permission for commercial use. Available for academic research.

name: Female Sexual Function Index
description: Nineteen-item self-report measure of female sexual function across six domains: desire, arousal, lubrication, orgasm, satisfaction, and pain. Widely used in clinical trials and population studies as the reference standard for female sexual dysfunction assessment.

scoring_method.note: Each domain multiplied by a weighting factor; total = sum of domains; cutoff ≤26.55 indicates sexual dysfunction

license_detail: Rosen et al. (2000). Copyright; requires permission.

name: Western Ontario and McMaster Universities Osteoarthritis Index
description: Twenty-four item patient-reported outcome measure for hip and knee osteoarthritis assessing pain (5 items), stiffness (2 items), and physical function (17 items). Available in Likert, VAS, and NRS response formats.

scoring_method.note: Normalized 0-100 versions also used; higher scores indicate worse condition

license_detail: Bellamy et al. (1988). Nicholas Bellamy. Free for academic and clinical use. Available at womac.org.

name: Health Assessment Questionnaire Disability Index
description: Twenty-item patient self-report functional assessment measuring difficulty in eight functional areas: dressing and grooming, arising, eating, walking, hygiene, reach, grip, and usual activities. The primary outcome measure in rheumatoid arthritis clinical trials.

scoring_method.note: Each category scored 0-3; overall disability index = mean of category scores, modified by aids/devices

license_detail: Fries et al. (1980). Stanford University. Requires permission for commercial use; free for academic research.

name: Disease Activity Score in 28 Joints
description: Composite disease activity measure for rheumatoid arthritis comprising tender joint count (28 joints), swollen joint count (28 joints), erythrocyte sedimentation rate or CRP, and patient global assessment of disease activity on a VAS.
license_detail: van der Heijde et al. (1990). Public domain; developed by the DAS-RA Working Group.

name: Ankylosing Spondylitis Quality of Life
description: Eighteen-item disease-specific quality of life questionnaire for patients with ankylosing spondylitis measuring the impact of the disease on daily activities, sleep, emotional function, independence, and relationships.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate worse quality of life; each item scored 0 (yes) or 1 (no)

license_detail: Doward et al. (2003). Copyright Galen Research. Requires licensing.

name: Protocol for Responding to and Assessing Patients' Assets, Risks, and Experiences
description: Twenty-one item social determinants of health screening tool developed by the National Association of Community Health Centers measuring personal characteristics, money and resources, social and emotional health, and optional additional questions about substance use and domestic safety.

scoring_method.note: No single composite score; each domain generates actionable flags for care coordination

license_detail: National Association of Community Health Centers (NACHC). Free for use with attribution.

name: Accountable Health Communities Health-Related Social Needs Screening Tool
description: Ten-item core social needs screening tool developed by CMS for the Accountable Health Communities model, assessing housing instability, food insecurity, transportation problems, utility help needs, and interpersonal safety.

scoring_method.note: Each item flags need (yes/no); supplemental questions available for additional social needs

license_detail: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services. Public domain.

name: American Society of Anesthesiologists Physical Status Classification
description: Single-item ordinal classification (ASA I–VI) used by anesthesiologists to estimate pre-operative physical health status and perioperative risk. The most widely used perioperative risk assessment tool in surgical settings worldwide.

interpretation.IV: Patient with severe systemic disease that is a constant threat to life
interpretation.V: Moribund patient not expected to survive without the operation

license_detail: American Society of Anesthesiologists. Public domain standard classification.

name: WHO Surgical Safety Checklist
description: Nineteen-item perioperative safety checklist developed by the World Health Organization for use at three critical phases of surgery: Sign In (before anesthesia), Time Out (before incision), and Sign Out (before patient leaves operating room).

scoring_method.note: Items marked complete/incomplete; no composite score; compliance rate tracked

license_detail: World Health Organization (2008). Freely available at who.int/patientsafety.

name: PROMIS Global Health-10
description: Ten-item global health measure from the NIH PROMIS initiative assessing general health perceptions, quality of life, physical health, mental health, social health, pain, fatigue, and emotional problems. Produces Global Physical Health (GPH) and Global Mental Health (GMH) T-scores.

scoring_method.note: T-scores standardized to US general population (mean 50, SD 10); 2 items not included in subscale scores

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

items.text: In general, would you say your health is:
In general, would you say your quality of life is:
In general, how would you rate your physical health?
In general, how would you rate your mental health, including your mood and your ability to think?
In general, how would you rate your satisfaction with your social activities and relationships?
To what extent are you able to carry out your everyday physical activities such as walking, climbing stairs, carrying groceries, or moving a chair?
In the past 7 days, how often have you been bothered by emotional problems such as feeling anxious, depressed or irritable?
In the past 7 days, how would you rate your fatigue on average?
In the past 7 days, how would you rate your pain on average?
In the past 7 days, how much did pain interfere with your usual activities?

name: PROMIS Profile-29
description: Twenty-nine item PROMIS profile instrument measuring seven health domains: physical function, anxiety, depression, fatigue, sleep disturbance, satisfaction with social roles and activities, and pain interference, plus a pain intensity NRS item.

scoring_method.note: Each domain scored as a T-score (mean 50, SD 10 in US general population)

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Physical Function
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-reported capability to perform physical activities, ranging from basic mobility to vigorous activities, available in 4-, 6-, 8-, or 10-item short forms or computerized adaptive testing.

scoring_method.note: T-score centered at 50 (mean of US general population, SD 10); higher scores = better physical function

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Pain Interference
description: PROMIS item bank short form assessing how much pain interferes with daily activities, work, leisure activities, social interactions, mood, and concentration, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or computerized adaptive testing.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more pain interference; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Fatigue
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-reported fatigue, including tiredness, energy depletion, and the functional and emotional impact of fatigue over the past 7 days, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more fatigue; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Emotional Distress — Anxiety
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-reported fear, anxious misery, hyperarousal, and somatic symptoms related to arousal over the past 7 days, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more anxiety symptoms; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Emotional Distress — Depression
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-reported negative mood, views of self, social cognition, and decreased positive affect and engagement over the past 7 days, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more depressive symptoms; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Sleep Disturbance
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-perceived quality and depth of sleep, difficulty falling asleep, and whether sleep is refreshing over the past 7 days, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more sleep disturbance; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Social Isolation
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring perceptions of being avoided, excluded, detached from, or unknown by others, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = more social isolation; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Cognitive Function
description: PROMIS item bank short form measuring self-perceived cognitive abilities including memory, concentration, speed of processing, and ability to perform complex tasks over the past 7 days, available in 4-, 6-, or 8-item short forms or CAT.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores = better cognitive function; T-score referenced to US general population

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Pain Intensity 3a
description: Three-item PROMIS Pain Intensity short form (version 3a) measuring current, average, and worst pain intensity over the past 7 days using 0-10 numeric rating scales.

scoring_method.note: T-score referenced to US general population; higher scores = more pain intensity

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: PROMIS Pediatric Measures
description: Suite of pediatric PROMIS short forms measuring physical function, pain interference, fatigue, depressive symptoms, anxiety, peer relationships, and asthma impact in children and adolescents ages 5-17, with parallel parent proxy versions available.

scoring_method.note: Pediatric norms separate from adult norms; T-score mean 50, SD 10

license_detail: NIH PROMIS. Free for use. Available at healthmeasures.net.

name: National Eye Institute Visual Functioning Questionnaire-25
description: Twenty-five item self-administered questionnaire measuring the influence of visual impairment and ocular conditions on health-related quality of life across vision-targeted functional domains and well-being, including general vision, near and distance activities, driving, peripheral vision, color vision, and ocular pain.

scoring_method.note: Higher scores indicate better visual function; composite score is mean of all vision-targeted subscale scores

license_detail: National Eye Institute (NEI). Public domain. Available at nei.nih.gov.

name: Yale Global Tic Severity Scale
description: Clinician-rated 12-item semi-structured interview assessing tic severity in Tourette syndrome and related tic disorders across motor and phonic tic dimensions (number, frequency, intensity, complexity, interference) plus an overall impairment rating.
license_detail: Leckman et al. (1989). Yale University. Free for clinical and research use.

name: Morisky Medication Adherence Scale-8
description: Eight-item self-report measure of medication-taking behavior assessing both inadvertent and intentional non-adherence across seven yes/no items and one 5-point Likert item, validated for chronic disease medication adherence in multiple conditions.

scoring_method.note: Items 1-7 scored 0 (yes) or 1 (no); item 8 scored 0-1 with fractional scoring

license_detail: Copyright Donald Morisky. Commercial licensing required at moriskyscale.com. Free for academic research with permission.

name: Patient Health Questionnaire — Somatic, Anxiety, and Depressive Symptoms
description: Multi-module PHQ instrument combining the PHQ-15 somatic symptom severity scale, GAD-7 anxiety module, and PHQ-9 depression module for comprehensive primary care mental health screening, enabling simultaneous assessment of somatic, anxiety, and depressive comorbidity.
license_detail: Public Domain — Pfizer Inc. PHQ instruments released into the public domain.

name: Quality of Life Scale — Single Item
description: Single global quality of life assessment item asking patients to rate their overall quality of life on a 5-point scale; sometimes expanded to a 6-item form to capture multiple life domains including physical, psychological, social, environmental, spiritual, and overall quality of life.

scoring_method.note: Single item or multi-domain variants depending on administration context

license_detail: Public domain. Referenced in LOINC panel 72355-1.
